name: Alabama School of Fine Arts
about: The Alabama School of Fine Arts is a tuition-free public specialty school in downtown Birmingham serving grades 7-12. It offers intensive pre-professional training in six departments: music, dance, theatre arts, visual arts, creative writing, and math and science. A residential program is available for students in grades 9-12 who live outside the Birmingham metro area.
notable_alumni: Suzanne Collins (author of The Hunger Games), Laverne Cox, Jennifer Hale, Mitski
financial_aid_notes: Tuition-free public school; in-state students pay no tuition. Out-of-state students are charged tuition.

name: Alabama School of Math and Science
about: The Alabama School of Mathematics and Science (ASMS) is Alabama's only fully public residential high school, serving sophomores, juniors, and seniors from across the state's 67 counties. All courses are taught at Advanced Placement or Honors level, and tuition, room, and board are free. The Class of 2024 posted an average composite ACT of about 28, with 100% of graduates matriculating to college.
college_matriculation: 100% of graduates matriculate to college; average of ~$133,000 in scholarships per graduate; 180+ National Merit Finalists to date
notable_alumni: Virgil Griffith (creator of WikiScanner), Ryan Williams (MIT computer science professor)
financial_aid_notes: Tuition-free public residential school; room and board are free. Only modest fees apply (annual activity fee ~$1,575 plus enrollment/testing fees).

name: Indian Springs School
about: Indian Springs School is a coeducational independent day and boarding school for grades 8-12, located on a 350-acre campus about fifteen miles south of Birmingham, Alabama. Founded in 1952, it emphasizes a 'learning through living' philosophy and enrolls students from multiple states and countries. Roughly a quarter of students board, including a significant international contingent.
notable_alumni: John Green (author), John Badham (director), Elaine Luria (U.S. Representative), David Y. Oh (NASA engineer)
financial_aid_notes: Need-based; ~30% of students receive aid; about $2.7 million awarded annually.

name: Southern Preparatory Academy
affiliation: Independent (military academy; member Alabama Independent School Association)
about: Southern Preparatory Academy is a college-preparatory military day and boarding school in Camp Hill, Alabama, on a 330-acre campus. Founded in 1898 by Dr. Lyman Ward (and formerly named Lyman Ward Military Academy), it operates a JROTC program and a corps-of-cadets structure. Historically boys-only for boarding, the school began welcoming female day students in the 2025-2026 school year.
accreditation: Southern Association of Colleges and Schools (SACS)
financial_aid_notes: Participates in Alabama's CHOOSE Act (education savings accounts) for eligible families; scholarships available.
clubs: Corps of Cadets (Alpha, Bravo, Echo companies)

name: St. Bernard Preparatory School
about: St. Bernard Preparatory School is a private Roman Catholic, coeducational day and boarding school for grades 7-12 in Cullman, Alabama, run by the Benedictine monks of the adjacent St. Bernard Abbey. Founded in 1891, it offers a liberal-arts college-preparatory curriculum and an ESL program for international students. The campus spans more than 800 acres.
accreditation: Southern Association of Colleges and Schools (SACS)
financial_aid_notes: Over $500,000 awarded annually; may assist with up to 70% of total school expenses for qualifying families.
head_of_school: Fr. Paschal Pautler, O.S.B. (President, 2026-2027); Fr. Joel Martin, O.S.B. (Interim Headmaster)

name: Mt. Edgecumbe High School
affiliation: Public (state-operated; Alaska Department of Education and Early Development)
about: Mt. Edgecumbe High School is a public, tuition-free boarding high school on Japonski Island in Sitka, Alaska, operated by the State of Alaska. Established in 1947, it draws students from roughly 110 villages and communities across the state, with a predominantly Alaska Native enrollment. Most students board on campus, and the great majority go on to college.
college_matriculation: About 90% of graduates attend college or university
financial_aid_notes: Tuition-free public boarding school; room and board provided by the State of Alaska.

name: Verde Valley School
about: Verde Valley School is an independent IB World School and boarding/day high school for grades 9-12 in Sedona, Arizona. Founded in 1948 as one of the nation's first international, multicultural boarding schools, it enrolls students from around eighteen countries. The International Baccalaureate is the sole curriculum in grades 11 and 12, and the school is known for its emphasis on intercultural understanding, horsemanship, and wilderness/field programs.
notable_alumni: James Horner (film composer), Chris Lemmon (actor), Anne Lockhart (actress), Benson K. Whitney (U.S. Ambassador to Norway), Andre R. Guttfreund (filmmaker)

name: Arkansas School for Mathematics, Sciences, and the Arts
about: The Arkansas School for Mathematics, Sciences, and the Arts (ASMSA) in Hot Springs is the only public residential high school in Arkansas and is part of the University of Arkansas System. It serves academically talented students in grades 10-12, who live on campus tuition-free. All courses are taught at honors level or above, and roughly 70 courses are available for university credit, with graduates earning an average of about 50 college credit hours.
college_matriculation: 100% college matriculation; graduates earn an average of ~50 college credit hours
financial_aid_notes: Tuition-free public residential school; the state covers tuition, and students from across Arkansas attend at no charge.
